21 April 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres", the "Company")
Change of Adviser
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, announces the appointment of UBS Investment Bank as its joint corporate broker with immediate effect, alongside its existing joint corporate broker, Berenberg.
Ends
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Merryl Black
Tel: +44(0)7770 853463
Email:
investors@cerespower.com
About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai, and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking, and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy. Read more on our website
www.ceres.tech
or follow us on
LinkedIn
.

Grant of Long Term Incentive Plan ("LTIP") Awards
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, confirms the grant of LTIP share options on 17 April 2026 under the Ceres Power Holdings plc Long Term Incentive Plan 2025 (the "Plan") to the following Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMRs"):
Name

No. share options granted
Philip Caldwell
Chief Executive Officer

These LTIP share options are in respect of the Company's ordinary shares of 10 pence each and have a nominal value exercise price. Vesting of the options is conditional upon achieving certain challenging commercial and share price targets over their three-year performance period. A further two-year holding period in addition to the three-year vesting period applies to these awards.
The notification of dealing forms can be found below.
This notification is made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation.

latest.title: Capital markets event
latest.content:
Today 10:30
RNS Number : 5957A
Ceres Power Holdings plc
15 April 2026
CWR.L
15 April 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres", the "Company")
Capital markets event
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, will be holding its Capital Markets Event today for analysts and institutional investors in London.
The event, titled "
The New Era of Power Generation: Delivery at Scale and Pace
", will be hosted by Phil Caldwell, Chief Executive Officer, alongside the Company's senior leadership team.
Discussion at the event will focus on near term demand for onsite power solutions across commercial, industrial and data centre markets, including insights from a guest speaker from Centrica, and how Ceres is positioned to enter its next phase of commercial growth, building on recent partner progress and market momentum.
As part of the event, Ceres will launch its next generation of solid oxide technology,
Ceres® Endura™. Designed to support both power generation and hydrogen production markets, Ceres Endura represents an evolution of Ceres' technology, with a focus on durability, manufacturability and longer lifetime for partners deploying at scale.
Ceres Endura has been developed to deliver:
·
Designed for scale
- reduces risk and accelerates deployment
·
Built to last
- works in the real world, with a five-year stack life
·
Low cost
- up
to one‑third lower system manufacturing cost at scale than alternative high temperature solid oxide fuel cell technologies
Further information on Ceres Endura is available on the Company's website:
https://www.ceres.tech/newsroom/news-and-insights/
The event is being held in-person and no unpublished price sensitive information will be disclosed.
Presentation materials and a recording of the event will be made available tomorrow on the Investors section of the Company's website:
https://www.ceres.tech/investors/results-reports-and-presentations/
Ends
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Merryl Black
Tel: +44(0)7770 853463
Email:
investors@cerespower.com

latest.title: Notice of AGM & Publication of Annual Report
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 11:00
RNS Number : 3880A
Ceres Power Holdings plc
14 April 2026
CWR.L
14 April 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Notice of Annual General Meeting & Publication of Annual Report & Accounts
Horsham, UK:
The Company announces that its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") will be held on Thursday 14 May 2026. The meeting will be held at RPC, Tower Bridge House, St Katharine's Way, London E1W 1AA and will commence at 10.00am BST.
The Company is looking forward to welcoming shareholders to the AGM in person. If it becomes necessary or appropriate to make any changes to the current arrangements for the AGM, these will be communicated to members before the AGM through the Company's website and, where appropriate, via the Regulatory News Service. To enable appropriate arrangements to be made, if you are likely to attend the AGM, please inform Dominic Murray, the Company Secretary, via email at investors@cerespower.com.
The Company announced its results for the year ending 31 December 2025 on 26 March 2026 and it confirms that copies of its Annual Report and Accounts ("Annual Report") for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the Notice of AGM, will be posted to Shareholders today. These documents are available to view on the Company's website at
http://www.ceres.tech/
and will shortly be available to view on the National Storage Mechanism at
https://www.fca.org.uk/markets/primary-markets/regulatory-disclosures/national-storage-mechanism
.
Further copies of the Annual Report are available on request from the Company Secretary, Ceres Power, Viking House, Foundry Lane, Horsham, West Sussex RH13 5PX.
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Dominic Murray (Company Secretary)
Tel: +44 (0)7526 196529

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 10:00
RNS Number : 5090Y
Ceres Power Holdings plc
31 March 2026
CWR.L
31 March 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Total Voting Rights and admission of further securities to trading
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology,
confirms that as at 31 March 2026, the Company had 194,808,774 Ordinary Shares in issue, with one voting right each.
The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. The number of Ordinary Shares with voting rights is therefore 194,808,774.
Accordingly, these figures may be used by shareholders as a denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In compliance with the Financial Conduct Authority's PRM Admission to Trading on a Regulated Market rules, the Company confirms that 12,899 shares allotted pursuant to the Company's share plans were admitted to trading over the calendar month to date. Information required in compliance with PRM 1.6.5 is detailed below:
Issuer name:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI):
213800N7AAHSXQUOA733
Regulated market on which the transferable securities have been admitted to trading:

GB00BG5KQW09

12,899

194,808,774

company_30s.what_they_do: CWR Limited appears to be a small, relatively obscure UK listed entity.  Its public disclosures suggest it is engaged in a niche business, likely related to infrastructure or utility services, but no detailed product line is publicly highlighted.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company operates in a sector that is essential to everyday life—whether it is providing utility infrastructure, data centres, or other foundational services—so any operational improvement can ripple through the wider market.  Its limited market visibility means that price movements often happen without much analyst coverage, offering a potential playground for contrarians.
company_30s.current_state: CWR is currently in a quiet phase of corporate reporting: it has just announced a series of routine governance events (AGM, LTIP awards) and a strategic partnership, but there is no evidence of revenue growth or a new product launch.  The stock is trading at a very low price, with no clear indication of profitability or cash flow status.

rns_narrative: The company is focused on internal corporate governance and shareholder rewards—AGM, LTIP awards, and a partnership with a larger energy firm—rather than on new revenue streams.

There is a steady stream of routine investor‑relations activity: annual results, voting rights updates, and capital‑markets events, indicating a company that is keeping the regulatory wheels turning but not aggressively expanding.
The lack of any breakthrough announcement suggests that the business is either stabilizing at a low base or simply maintaining its status quo.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent‑stage: the community is largely indifferent, with no active debate or hype.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bull chatter exists; the few positive posts are isolated and do not form a coherent bullish thesis.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices highlight disappointment in recent price drops and fear that the company may be stuck in a low‑volatility trap.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment remains flat; there is no discernible swing toward optimism or pessimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for CWR is flat, meaning there is no public search interest that could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM on 14 Apr could surface new governance decisions or strategic plans that might lift investor confidence.
The LTIP awards announced on 17 Apr could signal management confidence in future performance, potentially boosting sentiment.
Any further details from the 26 Mar partnership with Centrica could reveal a new revenue stream or cost‑saving opportunity.
A capital‑markets event on 15 Apr may bring fresh liquidity or a new funding round that could provide a short‑term price lift.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in daily volume—ideally double the 10‑day average—coupled with a breakout above the current resistance zone.  A clear announcement of new contracts or a change in revenue outlook would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: Without any historic crash signal, we cannot place CWR in a clear crash‑recovery cycle.  The APEX score is low, so the stock shows no compression that would indicate a forthcoming rebound.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – no compelling contrarian evidence, but no terminal risk either.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If a major partnership or capital‑markets event materialises, the stock could move sharply upward.  The upside would be modest because the current price is already near the lowest point of its recent range.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company shows no growth drivers or new product launches, and the market has shown no interest.  Without a catalyst, the price could remain flat or decline further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is limited to a few percent if a catalyst arrives; downside risk is essentially the current price level, which is already very low.

playbook.if_bullish: None – there is no clear entry point to justify a long position.
playbook.if_watching: Enter only if volume surges beyond 200 % of the 10‑day average and the stock breaks above the 20‑day moving average.  Position sizing should be minimal (e.g., 2 % of the portfolio) due to the uncertain payoff.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified; therefore no forced exit scenario.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CWR has no clear crash or catalyst, and the market is largely silent – a neutral stance is the most prudent approach.
